ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn entry covers chicken thigh as a food product; braised preparation and skinless specification are not separately coded. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers cooked chicken thigh; skinless and braised modifiers are not separately enumerated in the base hierarchy. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for braised skinless chicken thigh as a food product. Left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one bone-in braised chicken thigh without skin, weighing approximately 120 g edible portion, commonly served as part of a rice or noodle meal.

GI value is set to 0 as a numeric sentinel consistent with the not_applicable gi_category designation for pure protein-and-fat foods. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (75 mg/100 g) with a favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio (183 mg K : 75 mg Na)
hypertension.guidance: Braised chicken thigh without skin is suitable for hypertensive individuals at 75 mg sodium per 100 g; however, patients should request minimal soy sauce or salt in the braising liquid, as cooking sauces can substantially increase the sodium content of the final dish.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; high protein (25 g/100 g) supports satiety and glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: With zero carbohydrate and zero sugar, braised skinless chicken thigh produces no meaningful postprandial glucose excursion and is an excellent lean protein choice within a structured type 2 diabetes meal plan, provided the braising sauce does not contain added sugar or starchy thickeners.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate cholesterol (90 mg/100 g) and estimated saturated fat (~2.7 g/100 g); no trans fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Braised skinless chicken thigh is a moderate-concern food for cardiovascular disease patients due to its cholesterol content of 90 mg/100 g; individuals with established CVD should limit portion size to approximately 100–120 g per meal and pair with high-fibre vegetables to mitigate dietary cholesterol impact.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of omega-3 fatty acids, dietary fibre, and meaningful polyphenols limits anti-inflammatory benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Braised skinless chicken thigh does not meet the positive anti-inflammatory criteria for rheumatoid arthritis management; it should be paired with omega-3-rich foods such as fatty fish, leafy greens, or turmeric-based preparations to support an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Braised chicken thigh without skin is a cooked dark-meat poultry product prepared from the upper leg of the chicken, with the skin removed prior to or after braising, and served bone-in. The braising process involves slow, moist-heat cooking in a small amount of liquid — typically water, stock, or a light soy-based sauce — until the meat becomes tender and juicy. The result is a soft, succulent texture with a mild savoury flavour that absorbs the seasoning of the braising liquid. In Singapore and Chinese culinary contexts, this preparation is commonly encountered as a home-cooked dish or as part of economy rice (cai fan) stalls, often braised with soy sauce, garlic, and spices. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 195 kcal, 25 g protein, 9.79 g fat (with an estimated 2.7 g saturated fat), 0 g carbohydrate, 0 g sugar, 75 mg sodium, 183 mg potassium, 11 mg calcium, 1.42 mg iron, and 90 mg cholesterol. The zero-carbohydrate profile makes it suitable for glycaemic control in type 2 diabetes management. The low sodium content (75 mg/100 g) is favourable for hypertension, though braising sauces can significantly increase sodium. The moderate cholesterol level warrants portion awareness for cardiovascular disease patients. It is halal-certified, gluten-free, and dairy-free, making it broadly accessible across dietary requirements.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11010019, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). This is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. Source match type is exact.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo — this food is a pure protein-and-fat product with no carbohydrate content by composition. Sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as this is a meat product with no plant-derived fibre. Trans_fat_g is set to 0.0 as skinless braised chicken thigh contains negligible industrially produced trans fat. Saturated_fat_g of 2.7 g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for cooked chicken thigh meat without skin (NDB 05178), consistent with the HPB total fat value of 9.79 g/100 g. Monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo entry for this item; USDA FoodData Central values exist but were not used to override the primary HPB source to maintain source consistency. Water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Singapore Foodo for this record. Vitamin and mineral micronutrients beyond those reported by HPB (potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) are set to -1 as they are not available in the HPB source entry for this food. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g/100 g per HPB); gi_category set to not_applicable. The glycemic_index field is set to the numeric value 0 as a machine-readable sentinel for not-applicable status on a zero-carbohydrate food, consistent with the validator requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition. Braising sauce composition can significantly alter the sodium, sugar, and carbohydrate profile of the final dish; values reflect the plain braised meat only as reported by HPB.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g 0.0 (confirmed zero, meat product), sodium_mg 75.0, saturated_fat_g 2.7 (estimated from USDA FoodData Central, documented in notes), sugar_g 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB). All four required secondary fields present and numeric.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to numeric value 0 as a machine-readable sentinel for not-applicable status on a zero-carbohydrate food, satisfying the validator requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric. gi_category set to not_applicable. glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. gi_source documents the not-applicable determination referencing University of Sydney GI Database framework. The notes field explains the numeric sentinel rationale.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI provided for chicken thigh food product with closest_parent match type and gap note. FoodEx2 code A0CKP for cooked chicken thigh meat with closest_parent match type and gap note. SNOMED CT left null with honest gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, covers food identity, preparation method, texture, cultural context (Singapore economy rice, Chinese braising), clinical relevance (diabetes, hypertension, CVD), and key nutrients. common_user_queries and meal_context populated. ingredient_synonyms provided in English, Chinese, and Malay.

Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid arthritis included as additional condition.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft produced with full multilingual naming, health context, ontology, and GI handling.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'. Ensured glycemic_index was set to null for zero-carbohydrate food.

Iteration 3: Validator feedback indicated glycemic_index must be numeric. Changed glycemic_index from null to numeric value 0 as a machine-readable sentinel for not-applicable status. Updated gi_source notes and record notes to document this sentinel rationale. gi_category remains not_applicable.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record F11010019 Braised Chicken Thigh Without Skin passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8 on the Q1–Q8 scorecard. Key fix in iteration 3: glycemic_index changed from null to numeric 0 to satisfy the validator requirement that glycemic_index must be numeric; gi_category remains not_applicable and the sentinel rationale is documented in notes and gi_source. All zero and -1 values documented in notes. Singaporean multilingual naming complete across English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. All three required health conditions present with clinical guidance. Aliases consistent between flat array and structured object.
